[Monopoly/Monopoly: The Mega Edition - Wikibooks, open books for an open world](https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/Monopoly/Monopoly:_The_Mega_Edition)\n\n# Monopoly Mega Edition Rules\n\n## Starting Setup\nEach player begins with $2,500 (including a $1,000 bill) instead of the standard $1,500.[1][2] The board is 30% larger than standard Monopoly and features 37 properties with 8 additional streets (one per color group) and a new Gas Company utility.[5]\n\n## The Three Dice System\nYou roll all three dice on your turn: two white dice plus a speed die.[1][2] \n\n**Speed Die Results:**\n- **Mr. Monopoly**: Move according to the white dice, then move again to the next unowned property you can buy. If all properties are owned, move to the first property where you must pay rent.[1][2]\n- **Bus symbol**: Move according to the white dice, then either take a bus ticket or move to the nearest Chance/Community Chest space.[1][2]\n- **Numbers (1-3)**: Add all three dice together to move.[1][2]\n\n**Special Rules:**\n- Rolling triples (1-1-1, 2-2-2, or 3-3-3) lets you move to any space on the board without rolling again—triples don't count as doubles.[3]\n- Rolling doubles on white dice gives you another turn (use a bus ticket instead if you prefer).[2]\n\n## Properties \u0026 Buildings\nYou may build houses and hotels once you own all but one property in a color group.[1][2] Once you own all properties in a group with hotels on each, you can build **skyscrapers** for increased rent.[1][2] You collect triple rent on unimproved properties in a monopoly.[1][2]\n\nFor railroads, you can build a **train depot** ($100) that doubles rent—you don't need multiple railroads first.[1][2]\n\n## Bus Tickets\nYou have 16 bus tickets total. On any turn, you can use one instead of rolling dice to move to any space on the same board edge. Once used, tickets are out of play.[1][2]",

{
  "markdown": "Know how to play the \nClassic Monopoly® Game?\nJust read pages 2-4\nand you’re ready to play\nMonopoly®: The Mega Edition\nCONTENTS\nGameboard • 37 Title Deed Cards • Money and Banker’s Tray\nTokens • 3 Dice • 16 Chance Cards\n16 Community Chest Cards • 16 Bus Tickets\n32 Houses • 12 Hotels • 8 Skyscrapers • 4 Train Depots\nPlease Note: The tokens in this game are made of metal and may bend. \n If they do, carefully bend them back into shape.\nAges 8+ 2-8 Players\n2\nWhat’s New?\nIn Monopoly®: The Mega Edition\nMONEY\nEach player begins with $2500, including a crisp \nnew $1000 bill, in addition to the standard five each \n$1’s, $5’s, $10’s, six each $20’s, and two each $50’s, $100’s \u0026 $500’s.\nTURN ORDER\nRoll only the two white dice to determine your playing order. \nTHE DICE \u0026 HOW TO MOVE\nOn your turn, roll all THREE dice: the two\n white dice plus the new SPEED DIE. \n• If pips appear on all three dice, simply \nmove the total of all three.\n• If you roll a Mr. Monopoly, you get a bonus move. First, move according\nto the white dice, do what you would normally do on that space; then move\nagain to the next unowned property, which you may now buy. If you roll a Mr.\nMonopoly after all of the properties are owned, make a normal move according \nto the white dice, then move ahead to the first property on which you need to\npay rent. If all other players properties are mortgaged, stay where you are. \nNote: An opponent can’t build or trade until your Mr. Monopoly move is completed.\n• If you roll the Bus, first make a normal move according to the white dice,\nthen decide to either take a BUS TICKET for later use (usually a good idea if\nany tickets are left in the deck) or move again to the nearest Chance/\nCommunity Chest space in front of your token.\nDOUBLES \u0026 TRIPLES\nRefer only to the white dice when determining doubles. If doubles entitle you to \nroll again, you may use a Bus Ticket in place of rolling the dice. While in JAIL, roll \nonly the two white dice (if you pay beforehand to get out, roll all three dice). If \nyou roll TRIPLE 1’s, 2’s, or 3’s, move ahead to ANY space on the board. Do not roll \nagain. You do not go to Jail if you’ve rolled doubles twice before rolling triples.\n?\n?\n3\nNEW PROPERTIES \u0026 SKYSCRAPERS\nEach color group has one additional property. You may build \nhouses and hotels once you own all but one property \nin a color group. If you own all of the properties of a color \ngroup, and have built hotels on each, you may then build \nSKYSCRAPERS. Refer to the property’s Title Deed for the cost of a \nSkyscraper as well as the amount of rent you can collect. \nNote: you are entitled to collect TRIPLE rent on any unimproved property in a group once \nyou own all of its properties. You may not mortgage any property of a group until all houses \non it are sold back to the bank. If you acquire the final property of a group, after previously \nbuilding on its other properties, you must un-mortgage this property immediately (if \nmortgaged) and build it up according to the “even” building rule or, if you can’t afford to do \nso, you must redistribute existing houses among all properties of the group. You may need \nto break down hotels to do this. You may sell a Skyscraper back to the bank for half its value \nand revert to a hotel.\nRAILROADS\nYou may improve your Railroads by building a TRAIN\nDEPOT on it (cost: $100). A Train Depot doubles the rent\ndue for the Railroad. You don’t need to own multiple \nRailroads before building a Train Depot on one. Train Depots \nmay be sold back to the bank for $50 each.\nBUS TICKETS\nOn any turn, you may choose to use a Bus Ticket (if you have one) instead of \nrolling the dice. If so, move forward to any space on the same side of the board. \n(Corners are the final space on each side, as well as the first space of the next \nside.) If you use a bus ticket to move to a utility owned by an opponent, roll both \nwhite dice to determine your rent (4X, 10X or 20X depending on the number of \nutilities owned by this opponent). You can buy/sell/trade Bus Tickets with other \nplayers. Bus Tickets can be used to move to the “Go To Jail” space (thus putting a \nplayer in Jail). This can be a good strategic play. After using a Bus Ticket, give the \nTicket to the Banker, it is out of play for the remainder of the game. (There are 16 \ntickets in all. When they are gone, they’re gone!) Watch out! When an “All Tickets \nExpire” card is drawn, all other Tickets in the hands of the players are lost to the \nbank (they’ve expired). If a Bus Ticket or Mr. Monopoly roll causes you to land on \nor pass GO, collect $200 as usual.\n4\nNote: All Bus Tickets should be revealed as collected and kept face up.\nAdditional rules clarifications and answers to frequently asked questions may be found at: \nhttp://winning-moves.com/rules\nOTHER NEW SPACES\nAUCTION If you land here, pick an unowned property for the \nBanker to auction off. Later in the game, when all of the properties are \nowned, you must move ahead to the property on which you need to \npay the highest rent (the closer one, if tied).\nBIRTHDAY GIFT If you land here collect $100 from the Bank, or \ndraw a Bus Ticket for use on a later turn. Your choice.\nBUS TICKET Take a Bus Ticket and keep for use on a later turn. Do \nnothing if no Bus Tickets remain. \nMONOPOLY GAME \nRULES OF PLAY\nStandard Edition (for your reference)\nOBJECT\nThe object of the game is to become the richest player by buying, renting and \ntrading properties.\nPREPARATION\nPlace the gameboard on a table, and put the Chance and Community Chest cards \nfacedown on their allotted spaces on the board. Each player chooses one token \nto represent them while traveling around the board. Each player is given $1,500 in \ncash divided as follows: two each of $500s, $100’s and $50’s; six $20’s, five each \nof $10’s, $5’s and $1’s. All remaining cash and other equipment go to the Bank. \nStack the Bank’s cash on edge in the compartments in the plastic Banker’s tray.\n®\n5\nBANKER\nSelect as Banker a player who will also make a good Auctioneer. A Banker who \nplays in the game must keep their personal cash separate from that of the Bank. \nWhen more than five people play, the Banker may elect to act only as Banker and \nAuctioneer.\nTHE BANK\nBesides the cash, the Bank also holds all Title Deed cards, as well as buildings \nprior to use by the players. The Bank pays salaries and bonuses. It sells and \nauctions properties and hands out their proper Title Deed cards; it sells buildings \nto the players and loans cash when required on mortgages. The Bank collects \nall penalties, loans, and interest, and the value of all properties that it sells and \nauctions. The Bank never “goes broke.” If the Bank runs out of cash, the Banker may \nissue as much money as may be needed by merely writing on any ordinary paper.\nTHE PLAY\nStarting with the Banker, each player in turn rolls the dice. The player with the \nhighest total starts the play. Place your token on the corner marked “GO,” roll the \ndice and move your token, in the direction of the arrow, the number of spaces \nindicated by the dice. After you have completed your play, the turn passes to \nthe left. The token remains on the space occupied and proceeds from that point \non the player’s next turn. Two or more tokens may rest on the same space at the \nsame time.\nAccording to the space your token reaches, you may be entitled to buy a property–\nor be obliged to pay rent, pay penalties, draw a Chance or Community Chest card, \n“Go to Jail,” etc.\nIf you roll doubles, you move your token as usual, the sum of the two dice, and are \nsubject to any privileges or penalties pertaining to the space on which you land. \nRetaining the dice, roll again and move your token as before. If you roll doubles \nthree times in succession, move your token immediately to the “In Jail” part of the \n“IN JAIL - JUST VISITING” corner space (see JAIL).\n“GO”\nEach time a player’s token lands on or passes over GO, whether by rolling the dice \nor by drawing a card, the Banker pays them a salary of $200. The $200 is paid only \nonce each time around the board. However, if you pass GO on the roll of the dice \nand land two spaces beyond it on the Community Chest space, or nine spaces \nbeyond it on the Chance space, and you draw an “ADVANCE TO GO” card, you \n6\ncollect $200 for passing GO the first time and another $200 for reaching it the \nsecond time by instructions on the card.\nBUYING PROPERTIES\nWhenever you land on an unowned property you may buy it from the Bank at \nits printed value. You receive the Title Deed card showing ownership of this \nproperty; place it faceup in front of you. If you do not wish to buy the property, \nthe Banker auctions it to the highest bidder. The buyer pays to the Bank the amount \nof the bid, in cash, and receives the corresponding Title Deed card. Any player, \nincluding the one who declined the option of buying the property at the printed \nvalue, may bid. Bidding may start at any price.\nPAYING RENT\nWhen you land on a property owned by another player, the owner collects rent \nfrom you in accordance with the list printed on its Title Deed card. If the property \nis mortgaged, no rent can be collected. When a property is mortgaged, its Title \nDeed card is placed facedown in front of the owner (see MORTGAGES). It is an \nadvantage to own all of the Title Deed cards in a color group because the owner \nmay then charge triple rent for unimproved properties in that color-group. This \nrule applies to un-mortgaged properties even if another property in that color\u0002group is mortgaged. It is even more advantageous to have buildings on properties \nbecause rents are much higher than for unimproved properties. The owner \nmay not collect the rent if they fail to ask for it before the second player \nfollowing rolls the dice.\n“CHANCE” \u0026 “COMMUNITY CHEST” CARDS\nWhen you land on either a Chance or Community Chest space, take the top card \nfrom the deck indicated, follow the instructions and return the card face down to \nthe bottom of the deck. The “GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!” cards are held until used \nand then returned to the bottom of the deck. If the players who draw these do \nnot wish to use them, they may sell them, at any time, to another player for an \namount agreeable to both.\n“INCOME TAX/LUXURY TAX”\nIf you land one of these spaces, you must \npay the Bank the amount shown.\n7\n“JAIL”\nYou land in Jail when\n(1) your token lands on the space marked “Go to Jail”;\n(2) you draw a card marked “GO DIRECTLY TO JAIL”; or \n(3) you roll doubles three times in succession.\nWhen you are sent to Jail you cannot collect your salary of $200 in that move since, \nregardless of where your token is on the board, you must move it directly into \nJail. Your turn ends when you are sent to Jail. If you are not “sent” to Jail but in \nthe ordinary course of play land on that space, you are “Just Visiting,” you incur no \npenalty, and you move ahead in the usual manner on your next turn. \nYou get out of Jail by\n(1) rolling doubles on any of your next three turns; if you succeed in\n doing this you immediately move forward the number of spaces shown \n by your doubles roll. Even though you rolled doubles, you do not take\n another turn.\n(2) Using a “GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!” card if you have one.\n(3) Purchasing a “GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!” card from another player and\n playing it.\n(4) Paying a fine of $50 before you roll the dice on either of your next \n two turns.\nIf you don’t roll doubles by your third turn, you must pay the fine of $50. You then \nget out of Jail and immediately move forward the number of spaces shown by \nyour roll. Even though you are in Jail, you may buy properties from or sell them to \nother players, buy or sell buildings and collect rents.\n“FREE PARKING”\nA player landing on this space receives no cash, properties or rewards of any kind. \nThis is just a “free” resting place.\nHOUSES\nWhen you own all of the properties in a color-group you may buy houses from \nthe Bank and place them on those properties. If you buy one house, you may \nplace it on any one of those properties. The next house you buy must be placed \non one of the unimproved properties of this or any other complete color-group \nyou may own. The price you must pay the Bank for each house is shown on your \nTitle Deed card for the property on which you place the house. The owner still \ncollects triple rent from an opponent who lands on the unimproved properties of \ntheir complete color-group. Following the above rules, you may buy and place at \n8\nany time as many houses as your judgment and cash will allow. You must place \nthem evenly, i.e., you cannot place more than one house on any one property \nof any color-group until you have placed one house on every property of that \ngroup. You may then begin on the second row of houses, and so on, up to a limit \nof four houses to a property. For example, you cannot place three houses on one \nproperty if you have only one house on another property of that group. As you \nplace evenly, you must also break down evenly if you sell houses back to the Bank \n(see SELLING PROPERTIES).\nHOTELS\nWhen you have four houses on each property of a complete color-group, you \nmay buy a hotel from the Bank and place it on any property of the color-group. \nYou return the four houses from that property to the Bank and pay the value of the \nhotel as shown on the Title Deed card. You may place only one hotel on any one \nproperty.\nBUILDING SHORTAGES\nWhen the Bank has no buildings to sell, players wishing to buy them must wait for \na player to sell theirs back to the Bank. If there are a limited number of buildings \navailable and two or more players wish to buy more than the Bank has, the \nbuildings must be sold at auction to the highest bidder.\nSELLING PROPERTIES\nUnimproved properties (but not buildings) may be sold to any player as a private \ntransaction for any amount the owner can get. However, no properties can be \nsold to another player if buildings are placed on any properties of that color\u0002group. Any buildings so located must be sold back to the Bank before the owner \ncan sell any property of that color-group. Buildings may be sold back to the Bank \nat any time for one-half of their printed value. All houses on one color-group must \nbe sold one by one, evenly, in reverse of the manner in which they were built. All \nhotels on one color-group may be sold at once. Or they may be sold one house \nat a time (one hotel equals five houses), evenly, in reverse of the manner in which \nthey were built.\nMORTGAGES\nUnimproved properties can be mortgaged through the Bank at any time. Before an \nimproved property can be mortgaged, all of the buildings on all of the properties \nof its color-group must be sold back to the Bank at half price. The mortgage \nvalue is printed on each Title Deed card. No rent can be collected on mortgaged \n9\nproperties, but rent can be collected on un-mortgaged properties in the same \ngroup. In order to lift the mortgage, the owner must pay the Bank the amount of \nthe mortgage plus 10% interest.\nWhen all of the properties of a color-group are no longer mortgaged, the owner \nmay begin to buy back buildings at full price. The player who mortgages a \nproperty retains possession of it and no other player may secure it by lifting the \nmortgage from the Bank. However, the owner may sell this mortgaged property \nto another player at any agreed upon price. If you are the new owner, you may lift \nthe mortgage at once if you wish by paying off the mortgage plus 10% interest to \nthe Bank. If the mortgage is not lifted at once, you must pay the Bank 10% interest \nwhen you buy the property and if you lift the mortgage later you must pay the \nBank an additional 10% interest as well as the amount of the mortgage.\nBANKRUPTCY\nYou are declared bankrupt if you owe more than you can pay either to another \nplayer or to the Bank. If your debt is to another player, you must turn over to \nthat player all that you have of value and retire from the game. In making this \nsettlement, if you own buildings, you must return these to the Bank in exchange for \ncash to the extent of one-half the amount paid for them. This cash is given to the \ncreditor. If you have mortgaged properties, you also turn these properties over to \nyour creditor but the new owner must at once pay the Bank the amount of interest \non the loan, which is 10% of the value of the property. The new owner who does \nthis may then, at their option, pay the principal or hold the property until some \nlater turn, then lift the mortgage. If they hold the property in this way until a later \nturn, they must pay the interest again upon lifting the mortgage. Should you owe \nthe Bank, instead of another player, more than you can pay (because of penalties) \neven by selling off buildings and mortgaging properties, you must turn over all \nassets to the Bank. In this case, the Bank immediately sells by auction all properties \nso taken, except buildings. A bankrupt player must immediately retire from the \ngame. The last player left in the game wins.\nMISCELLANEOUS\nCash can only be loaned to a player by the Bank, and then only by mortgaging \nproperties. No player may borrow from or lend cash to another player.\n10\nWINNING TIPS\nby Matt McNally,\nformer US Monopoly Champion\n1. MASTER THE TRADITIONAL GAME OF MONOPOLY.\nIf you understand the core principles of the standard MONOPOLY game,\nyou’re well on your way to having a great time playing, and winning, \nMONOPOLY The MEGA Edition. \n2. BUY WISELY.\nIn the MEGA edition, the location of your color group is often of vital \nimportance. It’s best to acquire a color group located after one that may not\nbe developed (because its ownership is split between two or more \nopponents). Late in the game, these properties will likely be mortgaged and\nMr. Monopoly (on the speed die) will deliver many opponents to your group.\nThus, if you own the railroad (if any) that precedes this group, mortgage it.\nIn the hands of an opponent, this railroad would rarely be mortgaged \nbecause it would gain the benefit of Mr. Monopoly, short-circuiting visits to\nyour group.\n3. BUILD IMMEDIATELY.\nIn MEGA, you can build houses and hotels when you own all but one \nproperty of a group. Build them as soon as you can. Beat your opponents to\nthe punch in the housing boom.\n4. BUILD IN REVERSE SEQUENCE.\nWhen building unevenly, build your next house on the first property of a\ngroup. Why? The speed die will advance opponents’ tokens to the nearest\nrent. This will likely be the first property of a group. At a minimum, try to\nbuild three-houses each. Great rents start at this level.\n5. EMPLOY YOUR COLOR GROUP STRENGTH.\n• Browns and Light Blues: Build these properties to hotels and skyscrapers.\n Doing so will make your opponents’ visits cost them dearly, perhaps\n even fatally. In cash-rich games, create a housing shortage by only \n building to the four-house level. This may prevent opponents from \n developing more expensive properties, due to a lack of houses in the bank. \n• Purples, Oranges, Reds, and Yellows: These are the best properties\n to own. They’re hit frequently and have tremendous bankrupting power.\n Trade for one of these groups when there is little fear of being the first\n player to pay a crippling rent.\n11\n• Greens and Dark Blues: Develop them cautiously. They are very \n expensive. Do so when: 1) You acquire naturally the three green properties \n or two dark blue properties; 2) the preceding color group is split and will\n not be developed; or, 3) the game is slow to reach the trading stage (if so,\n lots of cash will be in play).\n6. DEVELOP ONE COLOR GROUP FIRST.\nMr. Monopoly will move tokens around the board quickly. Thus, it’s better\nto have one highly-developed group rather than two lightly-developed\ngroups. Build up a second group if you have fully developed your first group\nand have lots of surplus cash. Ideally, this group will be on the opposite side\nof the board from your first group.\n7. MORTGAGE ALL NON-ESSENTIAL DEEDS.\nOnce you’ve acquired a group, raise money from mortgaging in order to \ngenerate fearsome rents via houses, hotels, and skyscrapers. Mortgage \nlone deeds from other groups first. Next, mortgage colored pairs, then\nsingle utilities, then single railroads.\n8. CREATE “SPEED BUMPS.”\nCapitalizing on the rapid movement of tokens around the board is \nimportant. Mr. Monopoly will be rolled one time out of three on average,\nand late in the game he will bring about many rents. By mortgaging, and later\nunmortgaging the same property, you can set up and dismantle\n“speed bumps.” Speed bumps enable you to maneuver an opponent\ninto hitting your heavily-developed color group, or if placed adroitly, to \nprevent an opponent from going bankrupt to anyone but you.\n9. OWN TWO RAILROADS; BUILD TRAIN DEPOTS.\nBuild train depots only when you control at least two railroads. Doing so\nwill help you achieve a great return on this investment. This tactic will also\navoid the need to sell a depot (at half price, of course) if you have one \nrailroad and wish to include it in a trade. \n10. TAKE A TICKET; RIDE THE BUS.\nSave a bus ticket whenever possible, unless you anticipate an opponent\ndrawing a bus ticket that will cause all others to expire. Early in the game use\na ticket to land on an unowned property–especially one that will enable\nyou to form a color group. Late in the game, use a ticket to bypass \nopponents’ groups or to go to jail and delay the risk of paying a big rent. \n12\nThank you for purchasing MONOPOLY®: The Mega Edition.\nFor Questions or Comments contact us at:\nWinning Moves Games\n75 Sylvan St., Suite C-104\nDanvers, MA 01923\n800-664-7788 or 978-777-7464 ext. 114\nwmoves@winning-moves.com\nGo to www.winning-moves.com\nfor the latest MONOPOLY®: The Mega Edition\nnews and expert tips.\nLet us know what you think!\nThe MONOPOLY name and logo, the distinctive design of the game board, the four corner squares, the MR. \nMONOPOLY name and character, as well as each of the distinctive elements of the board and playing pieces, and \nHASBRO are trademarks of Hasbro for its property trading game and game equipment. © 1935, 2023 Hasbro. All Rights \nReserved. Under license to Winning Moves Inc., Danvers, MA 01923. Made in China. Colors \u0026 Parts may vary.\nFor more information about Winning Moves \nproduct safety and compliance please visit\nhttp://www.winning-moves.com/product_safety",
  "url": "https://winning-moves.com/images/Mega_Rules_2023.pdf"
}

BGG](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Guide_To_BoardGameGeek)\n- [Welcome to BGG](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Welcome_to_BoardGameGeek)\n- 1 }\"\n\nThe Hotness\n\n- List\n- Thumbnails\n- Hotness Display\n\nGames\n\n- Companies\n- People\n- Games\n\nBGG Store --\n\n## Monopoly Mega rules clarifications\n\n## Clarifications\n\nThese are rules clarifications for [Monopoly: The Mega Edition](https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/24764/monopoly-the-mega-edition). Its short rulebook gets you playing quickly, but is not a good reference for the various contingencies that come up in the game.\n\n| Note: When adding clarifications, please cite your source. |\n| --- |\n\n### Jail\n\nBeing sent to jail is preemptive, and always ends your turn.\n\n- If you roll the dice and roll a Bus icon on the speed die, but end up in jail from the white dice (either landing on the GO TO JAIL space, drawing a card or a third set of doubles), do you still get a Bus Ticket, or does your turn end before you get to draw one? NO BUS TICKET - TURN ENDS AS SOON AS YOU GO IN JAIL ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- When rolling to \"escape\" jail, you only roll the 2 white dice - not the special die - unless you pay to get out first. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- [Comments](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#toc12)\n\n### The Three Dice\n\n#### Rolling Triples\n\nRolling Triples (1-1-1, 2-2-2, or 3-3-3):\n\n- Also, if you roll triples, you \"move ahead\" to whatever space you choose, which means that if you choose to go to a space that is past go, you get $200.\n- You can move ahead to anywhere on the board. Triples do not count as doubles, and therefore will not send you to jail after two doubles. You do not roll again to move after triples. ([3](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References)) [Please note that this is a change in the official rules.] ([*](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#Comments))\n\n#### Rolling Doubles\n\nAs in [Monopoly](https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/1406/monopoly), rolling Doubles three times on a turn still sends you immediately to jail. Remember that Triples do not count as doubles. [Originally, triples counted as doubles; this was officially changed.]\n\n- Rolling Doubles on the White Dice and a bus on the third die would let you move the total number on the dice and then take a bus ticket ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References)) if you aren't now in jail.\n- Rolling Doubles on the White Dice and Mr. Monopoly on the third die would let you move the total number on the dice, and encounter the space. Then you would move according to the appropriate Mr. Monopoly rule, and encounter that space. Then roll all three dice again. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- Rolling Doubles on the White Dice and a different number on the third die would let you move the total number on the dice, encounter the space, and then roll all three dice again. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n#### The Bus\n\n- The rules state that when you roll the Bus Icon, you can take a bus ticket or move \"ahead to the nearest Chance or Community Chest space\". Do you get to pick which space you move to (i.e.: the nearest Chance space or the nearest Community Chest space). MOVE TO THE NEXT IMMEDIATE CHANCE/COMMUNITY CHEST SPACE. CHANCE AND COMMUNITY CHEST ARE TREATED AS THE SAME THING IN THIS CASE. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n#### Bus Tickets\n\n- Can bus tickets be used to move to the \"GO TO JAIL\" space (thus putting a player in jail?) ABSOLUTELY. THIS CAN BE A GOOD STRATEGIC PLAY. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- Can you buy/sell/trade Bus Tickets with other players? YES ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- If you use a bus ticket to advance to GO, you collect $200. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- If you roll the bus on the third die, you move according to the white dice first. If it's your third doubles on the white dice, you go to jail and end your turn immediately. i.e. No bus ticket for you! Otherwise, you then take a bus ticket, if any remain. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- Bus tickets which void other bus tickets that players hold must be announced right away and this part must be carried out, regardless of whether or not you decide to save or use the ticket right away. All Bus tickets should be revealed as collected and kept face up to verify. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n#### Mr. Monopoly\n\nIf you roll Mr. Monopoly on the third die, you move according to the white dice and encounter the space. You then move again according to the appropriate Mr. Monopoly rule. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n- If you roll doubles a third time, you go straight to jail as usual, and ignore Mr. Monopoly if rolled in that instance. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- If you roll doubles, you move according to the white dice and encounter the space, then you move according to the appropriate Mr. Monopoly rule as well. Then you roll all three dice again and move. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n### Property Improvements\n\nAcquiring a final title deed to complete a monopoly of a color, in which you already have improvements on the other properties, requires you to immediately build equal to (or one step above or below, per the \"even building\" rule) all the other properties on that color. If you cannot, then you must distribute houses equally, or hotels equally, or however low you must go. This might require you to sell a skyscraper (and get half price for it) in order to get a hotel, or to sell a hotel (and get half price for it) in order to get houses. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n- Situation: I own three of the green property and have three houses built on each. I obtain the fourth from another player (with no houses on it, of course). I must then build this property up evenly - or deconstruct my other properties down to match. Does this happen before or after the player currently moving finishes his/her turn? I.e.: Player lands on North Carolina Avenue and can't afford the rent with their liquid cash. So, instead of mortgaging or selling houses/hotels, they trade me Pennsylvania Avenue for the cost of the rent. Ideally, it's possible that the player could end up on Pennsylvania Avenue before their turn is up (either through a Rich Uncle Pennybags roll or if they rolled doubles on their last turn and roll double ones this time around) - so do I build up PA before they finish their turn, thus making them pay the extra rent, or do I wait until their turn is over only getting the \"triple rent\" for the property? YOU CAN'T BUILD UP ANY PROPERTY DURING ANOTHER PLAYER'S TURN. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- If I choose not to build the newly acquired property up, do I have to \"sell\" my other houses back to the bank (at half price) before re-building them on the new property, or can I just move my houses freely from property to property? YOU DO NOT HAVE TO TAKE DOWN ANY HOUSES TO MAKE IT EVEN. SINCE YOU ACQUIRED THE 4TH PROPERTY LATER AFTER THE HOUSES WERE BUILT, YOU'RE FINE AS IS. HOWEVER, YOU COULD NOT BUILD FURTHER ON THE EXISTING PROPERTIES UNTIL THE NEW 4TH ONE IS EQUAL. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\nRepairs: You pay the same for each skyscraper as you do for a hotel when you have the improvement card. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n- When a player draws the Community Chest card where the player is \"assessed for street repairs,\" the player pays $115 for each Skyscraper owned, and $25 for each depot. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- When a player draws the Chance card that requires the player to \"make general repairs,\" the player pays $100 for each Skyscraper owned, and $25 for each depot. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n### Utilities\n\n- The chance card that moves you to the nearest utility - it says you roll the dice and pay 10x the amount thrown - does this override the statement on the utility property that says you pay 3x the amount shown on the dice if you own all three? IT DOES NOT OVERRIDE ANYTHING. GO WITH WHAT IT SAYS ON THE UTILITY CARD. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- When you land on a utility, are you paying n× only the white dice, or all three? \"ONLY THE WHITE DICE\" ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- Do you roll the dice anew to determine your utility bill, or do you pay based on what was already rolled? As in [Monopoly](https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/1406/monopoly), \"DON'T ROLL AGAIN - YOU PAY BASED ON WHAT YOU ALREADY ROLLED. IF A CARD SENT YOU THERE, THEN YOU DO ROLL THE 2 WHITE DICE.\" ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n- You pay 4× the initial value of the railroad rent if you are forced to go to the nearest railroad via the chance card if it has a depot on it. In other words, the doublings are cumulative. ([2](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n### Auction\n\n- If a player lands on AUCTION or goes there as a result of the next unowned property via Mr. Monopoly and wishes to auction it, he automatically enters a bid of $1. Thus, if no other player bids against him , he will acquire the deed for $1. ([1](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications#References))\n\n## References\n\n1. [Official rulebook](http://www.winning-moves.com/images/megamonopolyrules.pdf).\n2. Anonymous from [Winning Moves](https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgamepublisher/59/winning-moves-games-usa).\n3. Joe Sequino, Vice President of Marketing for [Winning Moves](https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgamepublisher/59/winning-moves-games-usa).\n\n## Comments\n\n- [mark5ok](https://boardgamegeek.com/profile/mark5ok) contests the validity of this rule, writing, Okay, I CHALLENGE that rule!!! You state in your Official Rulebook that you only refer to the white dice when determining doubles, then you go on to say that you DON'T get to go again after rolling triples. Well, triples when refering to only the white dice IS STILL DOUBLES!!! You can't have it both ways!!!! I SAY KEEP triples as a doubles roll and allow people to take another turn!!! This is a situation that doesn't come up all that often anyway, so why not just keep a triples roll as counting as doubles??? That would make more sense and not CONTRADICT the \"determining doubles\" by the white dice rule!!!!!!!!!!!! - I'd like to hear other opinions on this matter... \"SHOULD\" triples count as a \"Doubles\" roll or not??? What do YOU Think???\n\n- [BT14](https://boardgamegeek.com/profile/BT14) I probably know the answer to this one. What happens if you have 3 of a group and you have houses on each and you then acquire the 4th by trade and the 4th is mortgaged. Are you then required to immediately unmortgage the 4th to keep even build?\n\n- [austinp44](https://boardgamegeek.com/profile/austinp44) When all properties are owned, and you roll My.Monopoly, you must go to the next property which you must [[[PAY RENT]]], so all you have to do is own one monopoly, mortgage all your properties except your monopoly and build hotels on them, and wait for someone to roll Mr. Monopoly, CHEAP!!!!\n- [TheDS](https://boardgamegeek.com/profile/TheDS) The even-housing-when-getting-the-fourth-property rule is self-contradicting. I can move houses, but I have to sell a hotel? Plus, he says if I have houses, I don't HAVE TO build new houses immediately, but if I have hotels, I DO have to. To be consistent with the way Monopoly works, then we should force unmortgaging (if applicable) AND force either buying new houses OR selling off excess to even them out. No house moving, and no uneven builds!\n\nHello\n\nI have few questions that are not clear in the rules booklet.\n\nCould you give some clarification on the following:\n\n1- When mortgaging properties, half of the price is returned, but when Un-mortgaging these properties, is any money lost? If so how much? Or do we merely pay the mortgage price stated on the back? (The same amount we mortgaged the property for.)\n\n2- We build houses, hotels and skyscrapers for the amount of money mentioned on the property cards. But do we sell it with the same amount? Or we lose money when breaking down houses? If so how much?\n\n3- This one is a bit complicated. Regarding the speed dice and especially Mr. Monopoly.\n\nSo the rule says, if you get Mr. Monopoly you advance to the nearest unowned property, if all are owned, you advance to the nearest property that you will pay rent on.\n\nSo the situation is like this\n\nPlayer A and player B are playing... all properties are either sold or mortgaged.\n\nPlayer A intentionally sells one of his properties before rolling the dice. This is to ensure that if he gets Mr. Monopoly when he rolls, he won’t be forced to pay rent to player B (thus escaping the above rule). He then goes to the property he just sold and rebuys it. Of course risking losing it if he didn’t get Mr. Monopoly. Is this ok? Is player A abiding by the rules? Player As argument has two parts: The first is that a player is free to buy, sell or build in his/her turn before or after rolling the dice. The second is that if every player does the same, this means the mortgaged properties are circulated and this gives a chance for players to expand their lands instead of just waiting for others to land on un-mortgaged properties.\n\nPlayer Bs argument is that Mr. Monopoly was meant to strengthen your position in the beginning of the game and bring you to your knees in the end.\n\nSo what is right and wrong here?\n\n4- For the same reason above, players are intentionally mortgaging the properties that have no buildings on them, so other players would land on properties with buildings if they get Mr. Monopoly. Is this ok?\n\nThese are the questions and I am happy to explain more if needed. I would appreciate your response about the right action for each request.\n\nRegards,\n\n| [Article](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications) [Edit](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/edit/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications)| [History](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/history/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications)| [Editors](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/editors/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications) |\n| --- |\n| [[What Links Here](https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/linksto/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications)] |\n\nX",
  "url": "https://boardgamegeek.com/wiki/page/Monopoly_Mega_rules_clarifications"
}

{
  "markdown": "How to play Monopoly - The Mega Edition | Official Game Rules | UltraBoardGames\n\n## Components\n\n- instructions.\n- tray\n- money\n- 1 speed die\n- 2 dice\n- 16 bus tickets\n- 16 chance and community chest cards\n- 37 title deeds\n- 10 tokens\n- 4 depots\n- 8 skyscrapers\n- 12 hotels\n- 32 houses\n- Quad-fold gameboard\n\n## What's different in this Edition\n\n## Money\n\nEach player begins with $2500, including a crisp new $1000 bill, in addition to the standard five each $1's, $5's, $10's, six each $20's, and two each $50's, $100's \u0026 $500's.\n\n## Game Play\n\nRoll only the two white dice to determine your playing order.\n\nOn your turn, roll all THREE dice: the two white dice plus the new speed die.\n\nIf pips appear on all three dice, simply move the total of all three.\n\nIf you roll a Mr. Monopoly, you get a bonus move.\n\nFirst, move according to the white dice, do what you would normally do on that space; then move again to the next unowned property, which you may now buy.\n\nIf you roll a Mr. Monopoly after all of the properties are owned, make a normal move according to the white dice, then move ahead to the first property on which you need to pay rent. If all other players properties are mortgaged, stay where you are.\n\nNote: An opponent can't build or trade until your Mr. Monopoly move is completed.\n\nIf you roll the Bus, first make a normal move according to the white dice, then decide to either take a BUS TICKET for later use (usually a good idea if any tickets are left in the deck) or move again to the nearest Chance/ Community Chest space in front of your token.\n\n## Doubles \u0026 Triples\n\nRefer only to the white dice when determining doubles. If\n\ndoubles entitle you to roll again, you may use a Bus Ticket in place of rolling the dice. While in JAIL, roll only the two white dice (if you pay beforehand to get out, roll all three dice).\n\nIf you roll TRIPLE 1's, 2's, or 3's, move ahead to ANY space on the board. Do not roll again. You do not go to Jail if you've rolled doubles twice before rolling triples.\n\n## New Properties \u0026 Skyscrapers\n\nEach color group has one additional property. You may build houses and hotels once you own all but one property in a color group. If you own all of the properties of a color group, and have built hotels on each, you may then build skyscrapers.\n\nRefer to the property's Title Deed for the cost of a Skyscraper as well as the amount of rent you can collect from having one here.\n\nNote: you are entitled to collect TRIPLE rent on any unimproved property in a group once you own all of its properties. You may not mortgage any property of a group until all houses on it are sold back to the bank.\n\nIf you acquire the final property of a group, after previously building on its other properties, you must un-mortgage this property immediately (if mortgaged) and build it up according to the \"even\" building rule or, if you can't afford to do so, you must redistribute existing houses among all properties of the group.\n\nYou may need to break down hotels to do this. You may sell a Skyscraper back to the bank for half its value and revert to a hotel.\n\n## Railroads\n\nYou may improve your Railroads by building a TRAIN DEPOT on it (cost: $100). A train depot doubles the rent due for the Railroad.\n\nYou don't need to own multiple Railroads before building a TrainDepot on one. Train Depots may be sold back to the bank for $50 each.\n\n## Bus Tickets\n\nOn any turn, you may choose to use a Bus Ticket (if you have any) instead of rolling the dice.\n\nIf so, move forward to any space on the same side of the board. (Corners are the final space on each side, as well as the first space of the next side).\n\nAfter using a Bus Ticket, give the Ticket to the Banker, it is out of play for the remainder of the game. (There are 16 tickets in all.\n\nWhen they are gone, they're gone!) Watch out! When an \"All Tickets Expire\" card is drawn, all other Tickets in the hands of the players are lost to the bank (they've expired). If a Bus Ticket or Mr. Monopoly roll causes you to pass GO, collect $200 as usual.\n\nNote: All Bus Tickets should be revealed as collected and kept face up.\n\n## Other New Spaces\n\n#### Auction\n\nIf you land here, pick an unowned property for the Banker to auction off.\n\nLater in the game, when all of the properties are owned, you must move ahead to the property on which you need to pay the highest rent (the closer one, if tied).\n\n#### Birthday Gift\n\nIf you land here collect $100 from the Bank, or draw a Bus Ticket for use on a later turn. Your choice.\n\n#### Bus Ticket\n\nTake a Bus Ticket and keep for use on a later turn. Do nothing if no Bus Tickets remain.\n\n## Monopoly - Standard Edition Rules\n\n## Object of the Game\n\nThe object of the game is to become the richest player by buying, renting and trading properties.\n\n## Setup\n\nPlace the game board on a table, and put the Chance and Community Chest cards facedown on their allotted spaces on the board. Each player chooses one token to represent him/her while traveling around the board.\n\nEach player is given $1,500 in cash divided as follows: two each of $500s, $100's and $50's; six $20's, five each of $10's, $5's and $1's.\n\nAll remaining cash and other equipment go to the Bank. Stack the Bank's cash on edge in the compartments in the plastic Banker's tray.\n\n## Banker\n\nSelect as Banker a player who will also make a Auctioneer. A Banker who plays in the game must his/her personal cash separate from that of the Bank.\n\nWhen more than five people play, the Banker may elect to act only as Banker Auctioneer.\n\n## The Bank\n\nBesides the cash, the also holds all Title cards, as well as buildings prior to use by the players.\n\nThe Bank pays to salaries and bonuses. It sells and auctions properties and hands out their proper Title Deed cards; it sells buildings to the players and loans cash when required on mortgages.\n\nThe Bank collects all penalties, loans, and interest, and the value of all properties that it sells and auctions. The Bank never \"goes broke\". If the Bank runs out of cash, the Banker may issue as much money as may be needed by merely writing on any ordinary paper.\n\n## Game Play\n\nStarting with the Banker, each player in turn rolls the dice. The player with the highest total starts the play. Place your token on the corner marked \"GO\", roll the dice and move your token, in the direction of the arrow, the number of spaces indicated by the dice.\n\nAfter you have completed your play, the turn passes to the left. The token remains on the space occupied and proceeds from that point on the player's next turn. Two or more tokens may rest on the same space at the same time.\n\nAccording to the space your token reaches, you may be entitled to buy a property-or be obliged to pay rent, pay penalties, draw a Chance or Community Chest card, \"Go to Jail\", etc.\n\nIf you roll doubles, you move your token as usual, the sum of the two dice, and are subject to any privileges or penalties pertaining to the space on which you land. Retaining the dice, roll again and move your token as before.\n\nIf you roll doubles three times in succession, move your token immediately to the \"In Jail\" part of the \"IN JAIL - JUST VISITING\" corner space (see JAIL).\n\n## Go\n\nEach time a player's token lands on or passes over GO, whether by rolling the dice or by drawing a card, the Banker pays him/her a salary of $200. The $200 is paid only once each time around the board.\n\nHowever, if you pass GO on the roll of the dice and land two spaces beyond it on the Community Chest space, or nine spaces beyond it on the\n\nChance space, and you draw an \"ADVANCE TO GO\" card, you collect $200 for passing GO the first time and another $200 for reaching it the second time by instructions on the card.\n\n## Buying Properties\n\nWhenever you land on an unowned property you may buy it from the Bank at its printed value. You receive the Title Deed card showing ownership of this property; place it faceup in front of you.\n\nIf you do not wish to buy the property, the Banker auctions it to the highest bidder. The buyer pays to the Bank the amount of the bid, in cash, and receives the corresponding Title Deed card.\n\nAny player, including the one who declined the option of buying the property at the printed value, may bid. Bidding may start at any price.\n\n## Paying Rent\n\nWhen you land on a property owned by another player, the owner collects rent from you in accordance with the list printed on its Title Deed card. If the property is mortgaged, no rent can be collected.\n\nWhen a property is mortgaged, its Title Deed card is placed facedown in front of the owner (see MORTGAGES). It is an advantage to own all of the Title Deed cards in a color group because the owner may then charge double rent for unimproved properties in that color-group.\n\nThis rule applies to un-mortgaged properties even if another property in that color-group is mortgaged. It is even more advantageous to have buildings on properties because rents are much higher than for unimproved properties.\n\nThe owner may not collect the rent if he/she fails to ask for it before the second player following rolls the dice.\n\n## Chance \u0026 Community Chest Cards\n\nWhen you land on either a Chance or Community Chest space, take the top card from the deck indicated, follow the instructions and return the card facedown to the bottom of the deck.\n\nThe \"GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!\" cards are held until used and then returned to the bottom of the deck.\n\nIf the players who draw these do not wish to use them, they may sell them, at any time, to another player for an amount agreeable to both.\n\n## Income Tax\n\nIf you land on the \"INCOME TAX Pay 10% or $200\" space you have two options:\n\nYou may estimate your penalty at $200 in cash and pay the Bank, or you may pay 10% of your total worth to the Bank.\n\nYour total worth is all your cash on hand, the printed values of mortgaged and unmortgaged properties and the cost of all the buildings you own.\n\nYou must decide which option you will take before you add up your total worth.\n\n## Jail\n\nYou land in Jail when\n\n- you roll doubles three times in succession\n- you draw a card marked \"GO DIRECTLY TO JAIL\"; or\n- your token lands on the space marked \"Go to Jail\";\n\nWhen you are sent to Jail you cannot collect your salary of $200 in that move since, regardless of where your token is on the board, you must move it directly into Jail.\n\nYour turn ends when you are sent to Jail. If you are not \"sent\" to Jail but in the ordinary course of play land on that space, you are \"Just Visiting\", you incur no penalty, and you move ahead in the usual manner on your next turn.\n\n#### You Get out of Jail By\n\nRolling doubles on any of your next three turns; if you succeed in doing this you immediately move forward the number of spaces shown by your doubles roll. Even though you rolled doubles, you do not take another turn.\n\nUsing a \"GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!\" card if you have one.\n\nPurchasing a \"GET OUT OF JAIL FREE!\" card from another player and playing it.\n\nPaying a fine of $50 before you roll the dice on either of your next two turns.\n\nIf you don't roll doubles by your third turn, you must pay the fine of $50. You then get out of Jail and immediately move forward the number of spaces shown by your roll. Even though you are in Jail, you may buy properties from or sell them to other players, buy or sell buildings and collect rents.\n\n## Free Parking\n\nA player landing on this space receives no cash, properties or rewards of any kind. This is just a \"free\" resting place.\n\n## Houses\n\nWhen you own all of the properties in a color-group you may buy houses from the Bank and place them on those properties. If you buy one house, you may place it on any one of those properties.\n\nThe next house you buy must be placed on one of the unimproved properties of this or any other complete color-group you may own. The price you must pay the Bank for each house is shown on your Title Deed card for the property on which you place the house.\n\nThe owner still collects double rent from an opponent who lands on the unimproved properties of his/her complete color-group. Following the above rules, you may buy and place at any time as many houses as your judgment and cash will allow.\n\nYou must place them evenly, i.e., you cannot place more than one house on any one property of any color-group until you have placed one house on every property of that group.\n\nYou may then begin on the second row of houses, and so on, up to a limit of four houses to a property. For example, you cannot place three houses on one property if you have only one house on another property of that group.\n\nAs you place evenly, you must also break down evenly if you sell houses back to the Bank.\n\n## Hotels\n\nWhen you have four houses on each property of a complete color-group, you may buy a hotel from the Bank and place it on any property of the color-group.\n\nYou return the four houses from that property to the Bank and pay the value of the hotel as shown on the Title Deed card. You may place only one hotel on any one property.\n\n## Building Shortages\n\nWhen the Bank has no buildings to sell, players wishing to buy them must wait for some player to sell his/hers back to the Bank.\n\nIf there are a limited number of buildings available and two or more players wish to buy more than the Bank has, the buildings must be sold at auction to the highest bidder.\n\n## Selling Properties\n\nUnimproved properties (but not buildings) may be sold to any player as a private transaction for any amount the owner can get. However, no properties can be sold to another player if buildings are placed on any properties of that color-group.\n\nAny buildings so located must be sold back to the Bank before the owner can sell any property of that color-group. Buildings may be sold back to the Bank at any time for one-half the amount paid for them.\n\nAll houses on one color-group must be sold one by one, evenly, in reverse of the manner in which they were built. All hotels on one color-group may be sold at once. Or they may be sold one house at a time (one hotel equals five houses), evenly, in reverse of the manner in which they were built.\n\n## Mortgages\n\nUnimproved properties can be mortgaged through the Bank at any time. Before an improved property can be mortgaged, all of the buildings on all of the properties of its color-group must be sold back to the Bank at half price.\n\nThe mortgage value is printed on each Title Deed card. No rent can be collected on mortgaged properties, but rent can be collected on un-mortgaged properties in the same group. In order to lift the mortgage, the owner must pay the Bank the amount of the mortgage plus 10% interest.\n\nWhen all of the properties of a color-group are no longer mortgaged, the owner may begin to buy back buildings at full price. The player who mortgages a property retains possession of it and no other player may secure it by lifting the mortgage from the Bank.\n\nHowever, the owner may sell this mortgaged property to another player at any agreed upon price. If you are the new owner, you may lift the mortgage at once if you wish by paying off the mortgage plus 10% interest to the Bank.\n\nIf the mortgage is not lifted at once, you must pay the Bank 10% interest when you buy the property and if you lift the mortgage later you must pay the Bank an additional 10% interest as well as the amount of the mortgage.\n\n## Bankruptcy\n\nYou are declared bankrupt if you owe more than you can pay either to another player or to the Bank. If your debt is to another player, you must turn over to that player all that you have of value and retire from the game.\n\nIn making this settlement, if you own buildings, you must return these to the Bank in exchange for cash to the extent of one-half the amount paid for them. This cash is given to the creditor.\n\nIf you have mortgaged properties, you also turn these properties over to your creditor but the new owner must at once pay the Bank the amount of interest on the loan, which is 10% of the value of the property.\n\nThe new owner who does this may then, at his/her option, pay the principal or hold the property until some later turn, then lift the mortgage. If he/she holds the property in this way until a later turn, he/she must pay the interest again upon lifting the mortgage.\n\nShould you owe the Bank, instead of another player, more than you can pay (because of penalties) even by selling off buildings and mortgaging properties, you must turn over all assets to the Bank.\n\nIn this case, the Bank immediately sells by auction all properties so taken, except buildings. A bankrupt player must immediately retire from the game. The last player left in the game wins.\n\n## Miscellaneous\n\nCash can only be loaned to a player by the Bank, and then only by mortgaging properties. No player may borrow from or lend cash to another player.\n\n## Other Monopoly Editions\n\n- [Monopoly Voice Banking](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/voice-banking-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Ultimate Banking](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/ultimate-banking-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Toy Story](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/toy-story-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Star Wars](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/star-wars-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Revolution](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/revolution-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Speed](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/speed.php)\n- [Monopoly The Simpsons](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/the-simpsons.php)\n- [Monopoly Stranger Things](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/stranger-things.php)\n- [Monopoly Socialism](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/socialism-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Peppa Pig](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/peppa-pig.php)\n- [Monopoly Original](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly - Longest Game Ever](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/longest-game-ever-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Marvel Avengers](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/marvel-avengers-game-rules.php)\n- [Ms. Monopoly](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/ms-monopoly-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Junior Party](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/junior-party-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Junior Electronic Banking](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/junior-electronic-banking-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Junior](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/junior-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Gamer](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/gamer-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Friends](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/friends.php)\n- [Monopoly Game of Thrones](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/game-of-thrones-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Frozen II](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/frozen-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly for Millennials](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/millennials-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Fortnite](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/fortnite.php)\n- [Monopoly Deal](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/deal-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Deadpool](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/deadpool-game-rules.php)\n- [Monopoly Cheaters Edition](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/cheaters-edition.php)\n- [Monopoly City](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/city-game-rules.php)\n- [Anti-Monopoly](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/anti-monopoly-rules.php)\n\n[Continue Reading](https://www.ultraboardgames.com/monopoly/city-game-rules.php)",

Here’s a summary of the **Monopoly®: The Mega Edition** rules based on the official 2023 Winning Moves rules and clarifications:

---

## **Money**

- Each player starts with **$2,500** (including a $1,000 bill), instead of $1,500.

---

## **The Three Dice (Speed Die)**

You roll **all three dice** each turn: two white dice + the Speed Die.

| Speed Die Result | Effect |
|------------------|--------|
| **Numbers (1–3)** | Add all three dice for your move. |
| **Mr. Monopoly** | Move with the white dice and resolve that space. Then move again to the **next unowned property** (you may buy it). If all properties are owned, move to the **first property where you must pay rent**. If all others’ properties are mortgaged, stay where you are. Opponents cannot build or trade until your Mr. Monopoly move is finished. |
| **Bus** | Move with the white dice. Then either **take a Bus Ticket** or move to the **nearest Chance/Community Chest** space ahead of you. |

---

## **Doubles & Triples**

- **Doubles** are determined only by the two white dice.
- Rolling doubles gives you another roll; you may use a Bus Ticket instead of rolling.
- **Triples (1-1-1, 2-2-2, or 3-3-3)**: Move to **any space** on the board. You do **not** roll again. Triples do **not** count as doubles (no jail for “three doubles”).
- In jail, roll only the two white dice to get out (unless you pay $50 first, then roll all three).

---

## **Properties & Buildings**

- **37 properties** (one extra per color group) and a **Gas Company** utility.
- You may build houses and hotels once you own **all but one** property in a color group.
- **Triple rent** on unimproved properties when you own all properties in that color group.
- **Skyscrapers**: After hotels on all properties in a group, you may build skyscrapers (cost and rent on each Title Deed).
- Skyscrapers can be sold back to the bank for half their value, reverting to a hotel.
- If you complete a monopoly by acquiring the last property and already have buildings on the others, you must immediately unmortgage (if needed) and build evenly, or redistribute houses evenly.

---

## **Railroads**

- You may build a **Train Depot** ($100) on any railroad; it **doubles** that railroad’s rent.
- You do **not** need to own multiple railroads to build a depot.
- Depots can be sold back to the bank for $50.

---

## **Bus Tickets**

- **16 Bus Tickets** total.
- On any turn, you may use one instead of rolling to move to **any space on the same side** of the board (corners count as the last space of that side).
- Used tickets go to the bank and are out of play.
- Bus Tickets can be **bought, sold, or traded** between players.
- You can use a Bus Ticket to land on **Go to Jail**.
- Passing or landing on GO with a Bus Ticket or Mr. Monopoly pays $200.
- **“All Tickets Expire”** card: all Bus Tickets held by players are lost.
- Keep collected Bus Tickets face up.

---

## **Special Spaces**

| Space | Effect |
|-------|--------|
| **Auction** | Choose an unowned property for the banker to auction. If all are owned, move to the property where you owe the highest rent (closer one if tied). |
| **Birthday Gift** | Take $100 from the bank **or** draw a Bus Ticket. |
| **Bus Ticket** | Draw a Bus Ticket (do nothing if none remain). |

---

## **Utilities**

- **Electric Company, Water Works, Gas Company** (3 utilities).
- Rent is based on **only the two white dice** (not the Speed Die).
- If a card sends you to a utility, roll the two white dice to determine rent.

---

## **Repairs (Chance/Community Chest)**

- **Chance (General Repairs)**: $100 per Skyscraper, $25 per Train Depot.
- **Community Chest (Street Repairs)**: $115 per Skyscraper, $25 per Train Depot.

---

## **Clarifications (BoardGameGeek)**

- Going to jail ends your turn immediately; you do **not** get a Bus Ticket if you would have drawn one that turn.
- On the Bus, move to the **next** Chance or Community Chest space (they are treated the same).
- On Auction, if you land there and want to auction a property, you automatically bid $1 (so you can get it for $1 if no one else bids).
- You cannot build during another player’s turn.

---

Official rules and updates: [winning-moves.com/rules](http://winning-moves.com/rules)  
Official 2023 rules PDF: [winning-moves.com/images/Mega_Rules_2023.pdf](https://winning-moves.com/images/Mega_Rules_2023.pdf)

---

Here’s what I found:

## Official rules

The official Mega Monopoly rules say there are **16 bus tickets** in total and that **some** of them say “ALL TICKETS EXPIRE, EXCEPT THIS ONE.” They do **not** specify how many are regular vs. “expiration” tickets.

## Your app’s setup

In your monopolytracker app, the split is:

- **13 regular bus tickets** (b1–b13)
- **3 Big Bus tickets** (bb1, bb2, bb3) — the “All Tickets Expire, Except This One” type

Total: **16 tickets**.

## Summary

| Type        | Count | Effect                                                                 |
|-------------|-------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Regular** | 13    | Grants 1 bus ticket to the player who draws it                         |
| **Big Bus** | 3     | Clears all other players’ bus tickets; the drawer keeps this one       |

The 13/3 split is a reasonable implementation; the physical game’s rules don’t give an exact breakdown. If you have the physical game, you can confirm by counting how many tickets say “ALL TICKETS EXPIRE, EXCEPT THIS ONE.”
